title: The Fake Price That Makes You Buy the Expensive One
hook: Stores add a fake middle option just to make the priciest choice feel like a steal — and MIT proved it triples premium sales.
angle: The decoy effect (asymmetric dominance) is a documented cognitive bias systematically weaponized in pricing — Dan Ariely's Economist subscription experiment is the canonical proof with precise numbers
audience: Anyone who subscribes to streaming services, gyms, or software — or shops anywhere with three pricing tiers
key_numbers: 84% chose the expensive bundle when the decoy was present
Only 32% without the decoy — it nearly tripled premium sales
$2,800/year average extra spending from decoy-influenced purchases
73% of subscription services use 3-tier pricing intentionally
